body:
Title: CRA 2025 non-resident Schedules A, B, and C.

Sources:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5013-sa/5013-sa-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5013-sb/5013-sb-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5013-sc/5013-sc-25e.txt

The number 100 is used as the percentage multiplier on Schedule B line 3. The number 90 is used as the full-credit threshold on Schedule B. The decimal rate 0.145 is the 14.5% applicable rate on Schedule B.

Schedule A 2025 Statement of World Income: line 1 is net income from line 23600 of the return. Lines 2, 3, and 4 are Canadian-source net interest and other investment income, net rental income, and other Canadian-source income. Line 5 adds lines 1 to 4. Lines 6 to 12 are foreign-source net employment income, net pension income, net interest and investment income, taxable capital gains, net rental income, net business income, and other foreign-source income. Line 13 adds lines 6 to 12. Line 14 and line 16700 add line 5 and line 13 as net world income. Line 15 is the total of deductions from lines 24900 to 25600 that apply only to Canadian-source employment income, business income, and taxable capital gains from disposing of taxable Canadian property. Line 16 and line 16900 subtract line 15 from line 14 and enter zero if negative.

Schedule B 2025 Allowable Amount of Federal Non-Refundable Tax Credits: line 1 is net income from line 23600. Line 2 is net world income from line 14 of Schedule A. Line 3 divides line 1 by line 2 and multiplies by 100. If line 3 is 90% or more, enter the amount from line 35000 of the return on line 127. Part A line 4 totals lines 30800, 31000, 31200, 31217, 31600, 31900, and 32300. Line 5 is the applicable rate 14.5%, encoded as 0.145. Line 6 multiplies line 4 by line 5. Line 7 totals lines 34900 and 34990. Line 8 adds line 6 and line 7 and enters the amount on line 127. Part B line 9 is total federal non-refundable tax credits from line 35000. Line 10 totals lines 31220, 31240, 31270, 31285, 31300, and 31900. Line 11 is the applicable rate 14.5%, encoded as 0.145. Line 12 multiplies line 10 by line 11. Line 13 subtracts line 12 from line 9. Line 14 is amount eligible for section 217 election from line 13300 of Schedule C. Line 15 is the applicable rate 14.5%, encoded as 0.145. Line 16 multiplies line 14 by line 15. Line 17 is the lesser of line 13 and line 16 and is entered on line 127.

Schedule C 2025 Electing under Section 217: Part 1 lines 1 to 14 multiply eligible section 217 income in column 1 by the rate in column 2 and enter the result in column 3. Line 15 totals column 1 on line 13300 and column 3 on line 44300. Line 16 is total eligible section 217 income from line 13300. Line 17 is deductions from lines 20700, 20800, 20805, 23200, 25000, and 25600 that apply only to eligible section 217 income. Line 18 and line 14800 subtract line 17 from line 16 and enter zero if negative. Part 2 line 19 is net world income after adjustments from Schedule A line 16. Line 20 is taxable income from line 26000. Line 21 subtracts line 20 from line 19 and enters zero if negative. Line 22 is line 41700 of the return. Line 23 is the Canadian journalism labour tax credit from box 236 of T5013 slips. Line 24 subtracts line 23 from line 22 and enters zero if negative. Line 25 is the return of fuel charge proceeds to farmers tax credit. Line 26 subtracts line 25 from line 24 and enters zero if negative. Line 27 multiplies line 21 by line 26. Line 28 is line 19. Line 29 divides line 27 by line 28 and enters the section 217 tax adjustment on line 41450.
